methods.description: This method involves using palynomorph colors to infer the degree of maturation, as colors vary with temperature, burial depth, and maturation time. The colors are transferred to values according to the spore coloration index (SCI) or the thermal alteration index (TAI) schemes.

steps.description: Colors of thin-walled palynomorphs such as psilate trilete Cyathidites spores are transferred to values according to the spore coloration index (SCI) or the thermal alteration index (TAI) schemes.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert palynomorph colors to SCI or TAI values

steps.description: TAI can be calculated by visual examination of thin-walled palynomorph colors under light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate TAI by examining palynomorph colors under light microscopy

steps.description: SCI is a color gradation that varies from colorless or pale yellow to black; corresponding TAI is calculated using a five-point scale and the colors of spores and pollen before oxidation treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate SCI and TAI using a five-point scale

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the relative numeric frequencies (RNF %) of palynofacies assemblages using liptinite-vitrinite-inertinite (LVI) and vitrinite/huminite-liptinite-inertinite categories, presented as ternary plots to infer fields of the corresponding types of hydrocarbons produced.

steps.description: Relative numeric frequencies (RNF %) of palynofacies assemblages are analyzed using liptinite-vitrinite-inertinite (LVI) and vitrinite/huminite-liptinite-inertinite categories.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze RNF % of palynofacies assemblages using LVI and vitrinite categories

steps.description: Results are presented as ternary plots to infer fields of the corresponding types of hydrocarbons produced.
steps.searchable_summary: Create ternary plots to infer hydrocarbon types
